title: Buildah is not available for Ubuntu 22
body:
**Description**

GitHub Actions has added Ubuntu 22.04 LTS virtual environment: https://github.com/actions/virtual-environments/blob/main/images/linux/Ubuntu2204-Readme.md. However, unlike with Ubuntu 20.04 (https://github.com/actions/virtual-environments/blob/main/images/linux/Ubuntu2004-Readme.md), `buildah`, `podman`, and `skopeo` are not available.

Issue https://github.com/actions/virtual-environments/issues/5490 explicitly lists `buildah`, `podman`, and `skopeo` as missing in the "Software difference between Ubuntu 20 and Ubuntu 22" with note "Buildah is not available for Ubuntu 22 at the moment".

Please make Buildah available for Ubuntu 22 so that `ubuntu-22.04` environment can be used in GitHub Actions workflows that use `podman`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. In a workflow that works on `ubuntu-20.04` and uses `podman`, change
```diff
-    runs-on: ubuntu-20.04
+    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
```
for example https://github.com/adelton/redhat-entitlements/runs/6516332053?check_suite_focus=true.

**Describe the results you received:**

```
line 8: podman: command not found
```


**Describe the results you expected:**

Podman is installed on `ubuntu-22.04` GitHub Actions virtual environment and works.

Please provide Ubuntu 22.04-installable buildah and friends so that it can be included in `ubuntu-22.04`.

comments.body: Hi @adelton, Do you need `podman` or `buildah`. The issue is opened against `buildah` repo but your command is actually looking for `podman`. But anyways issue is quite similar to https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/4006. I think @lsm5 can answer this better.

I care about `podman` specifically. The reason why I filed this against `buildah` is because that's what https://github.com/actions/virtual-environments/issues/5490 uses as a justification for not installing `podman` on `ubuntu-22.04`.
Hi @adelton , github actions has gotten rid of container tools now, https://github.com/actions/virtual-environments/pull/5580 . AFAICT, this should not be an issue anymore. Closing..

Hello @lsm5, I'm a bit confused. That PR https://github.com/actions/virtual-environments/pull/5580 says

> Given that OpenSuse is not going to provide its repo to host podman and co. anymore lets remove these packages from Ubuntu older than 22.04

Does it mean that podman + buildah will no longer be present on any `ubuntu-*` GitHub virtual environment, or that they will get installed from stock Ubuntu repositories, on all `ubuntu-*` variants?

Could you chime in in that https://github.com/actions/virtual-environments/issues/5490 to point out to the correct approach to bring those packages to `ubuntu-22.04`?
@adelton responded. podman, buildah and skopeo are in the base 22.04 repos.
@adelton in case you also need to use the older ubuntu 20.04 and 18.04, buildah is back on the kubic repos. Sorry about the hassle.
